name: Tempura,japanese Way
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp mirin (sweet rice wine) or 1 tbsp sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a deep, thick-walled pan (wok is suitable) filled with 1 inch of peanut oil heated to the proper temperature: 340°F for vegetables or 360°F for fish.
2. Slice vegetables thin and fry in small batches; never crowd the pan.
3. Prepare the batter by beating the egg with water, then mixing in flour and whisking quickly until smooth.
4. For the dipping sauce, bring dashi no moto to a boil in water for 2-3 minutes, then turn off heat and add mirin (or sugar), sake, and soy sauce.
5. Prepare vegetables or fish by cutting into rings, strips, or cubes. For fish, dredge in flour before dipping in batter; dip vegetables directly into the batter.
6. Let excess batter drip off before lowering cooked vegetables or fish into the oil.
7. Cook until lightly browned, then remove and serve with tentsuyu sauce alongside rice or Japanese noodles (soba).
